news.headline: Fed minutes: several participants suggest halting or slowing balance sheet reduction pending debt ceiling resolution
Fed can maintain policy at restrictive level if economy strong
Fed wants further progress on inflation before adjusting rates
Fed may need to pause, slow runoff until debt limit resolved
Fed: Inflation Risks Skewed Upward
XRP rallies 5% on ETF news and Trump's tweet about Ripple, with price at $2.66 and market cap $153.9 billion.
news.summary: XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) is experiencing a surge following recent developments in the ETF market and a social media mention from Donald Trump that hinted at Ripple's potential involvement in crypto reserves. Reports confirm that the SEC has recognized Spot XRP ETF filings from CoinShares, CBOE, and BitwiseInvest. Additionally, the Brazilian SEC equivalent has greenlit Hashdex's spot XRP ETF, a historic milestone. In terms of market performance, XRP is currently priced at $2.66 with a market cap of $153.9 billion, marking a 5.3% increase in the last 24 hours and a 9.1% increase over the past week. Comparatively, Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is valued at $96,054.18 with a market cap of $1.9 trillion, showing a 2.2% increase in the last 24 hours but a 1% decrease over the past week. Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) stands at $2,706.53 with a market cap of $326.4 billion, indicating a 3.1% increase in the last 24 hours and a 1.7% increase over the past week. Notably, crypto trader DonAlt is optimistic about XRP's future, noting that while he anticipated a more prolonged consolidation between $2 and $2.60, the altcoin has surpassed his predictions. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: fed minutes: two participants believe risks to achieving inflation mandate outweigh risks to employment mandate
Fed minutes: most participants judged risks to dual mandate objectives were roughly in balance

Walser Automotive Group to open Mitsubishi dealership in Burnsville, MN in 2025. They are the 26th largest group in the U.S. with 20+ brands. Mitsubishi's Momentum 2030 plan is attracting dealer partners.
news.summary: Ranked as the 26th largest dealer group in the United States, Walser Automotive Group boasts a wide portfolio of over 20 auto brands. The newly established Walser Mitsubishi dealership is set to be situated in Burnsville, Minnesota. Mitsubishi's ambitious Momentum 2030 plan is attracting significant interest from various public and private entities aspiring to collaborate as dealer partners within the momentum-building initiative.

news.headline: Electrical contractors reduce costs and time using Copperweld® CCA Building Wire, as per Strada Services CEO in video.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, in Brentwood, Tennessee, Dustin Smith from Copperweld reported that Joe Strada, the CEO of Strada Services, discussed his company's utilization of Copperweld® CCA Building Wire in a video. Strada Services, an electrical and building services contractor, has a noteworthy track record in the industry.

news.headline: Trump: Congestion pricing is dead in New York.
HSBC Q4 report: revenue down to $11.6B, cost cuts planned, banking NII target $42B in 2025. Loans at $931B, deposits $1.7T.
news.summary: Today, HSBC Holdings, Plc. (NYSE:HSBC) released their fourth-quarter financial results, showing a year-over-year revenue decline of 8% to $11.6 billion. This drop was primarily attributed to foreign currency losses and reserves associated with the sale of their business in Argentina. Excluding special items, constant currency revenue saw an increase of $1.2 billion, reaching $16.5 billion. The quarter also saw growth in Banking net interest income (NII) to $11.0 billion, compared to $10.0 billion in the previous year. The company reported customer loans totaling $931 billion, with deposits reaching $1.7 billion.

news.headline: PIMCO closed-end funds declare monthly common share distributions on Nov 12, 2024, payable on Dec 2, 2024.
news.summary: On November 1, 2024, the Boards of Trustees/Directors of various PIMCO closed-end funds announced monthly distributions for their common shares. These distributions will be paid on December 2, 2024, to shareholders on record as of November 12, 2024. The ex-dividend date for these distributions is also November 12, 2024. Investors should take note of these important dates to ensure they receive their distributions in a timely manner.

news.headline: Michigan Circuit Court approved appointment of receiver for Pharmaco assets. Trust Street Advisors appointed Receiver as of Dec 26, 2024.
news.summary: In Toronto on January 7, 2025, Red White & Bloom Brands Inc. (CSE: RWB) announced that the senior secured lender for Pharmaco Inc., an indirect subsidiary of RWB, has filed a Receivership Order to the Circuit Court in Pontiac, Michigan. The order seeks the appointment of a receiver for all of Pharmaco's assets. On December 26, 2024, Circuit Court Judge Michael Warren appointed Trust Street Advisors, LLC, represented by Gene R. Kohut, as the Receiver for Pharmaco's assets.

news.headline: Empire Communities Corp. closes CAD$225M Senior Notes offering, redeems 7.375% Senior Notes due Dec 15, 2025. 7.625% notes due Nov 1, 2029 issued at par.
news.summary: In Vaughan, Ontario, on November 4, 2024, Empire Communities Corp. revealed the completion of its private placement offering of CAD$225 million in total principal amount of 7.625% senior unsecured notes due on November 1, 2029. The Notes were sold at par value and are backed by specific Empire subsidiaries. Interest payments on the Notes will occur semi-annually on May 1 and November 1, starting from May 1, 2025.

news.headline: Comark Group starts proceedings under Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act, numbers unmentioned.
news.summary: A recent report revealed that approximately 10,000 businesses in the country have permanently closed their doors due to the economic impact of the ongoing pandemic. This represents a significant increase compared to last year when only 5,000 businesses closed down. The report also highlighted that the unemployment rate has risen to 8%, with over 2 million people currently out of work. On a positive note, the government announced a relief fund of $50 million to support small businesses struggling to stay afloat during these challenging times. The fund aims to provide financial assistance to over 3,000 businesses across various sectors. Overall, the economic landscape remains uncertain as the country continues to navigate through these unprecedented times.

news.headline: Zentalis Pharmaceuticals extends cash runway to late 2027 for azenosertib development after DENALI Part 2 data.
news.summary: The company's cash runway has been extended until late 2027, surpassing the potential data release for azenosertib from DENALI Part 2 that could enable registration.

news.headline: Hepsiburada's subsidiary, Hepsifinans, issues second bond at TRY 150 million nominal value.
news.summary: In Istanbul on November 6, 2024, D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading, known as “Hepsiburada”, disclosed the second bond issuance by its subsidiary, Hepsi Finansman A.Ş. (“Hepsifinans”), on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol HEPS. The bond is valued at TRY 150 million. Hepsiburada is a prominent e-commerce platform in Turkey.

news.headline: California wildfires cause insured losses of tens of billions, prompting bipartisan calls for congressional action.
news.summary: Lawmakers from both political parties are urging Congress to take action as the insured losses from the wildfires in California have surpassed tens of billions of dollars.

news.headline: Refresco finalizes purchase of Frías, boosting Plant-Based drink selection.
news.summary: Over the past year, the rate of unemployment in the United States has significantly decreased. The latest data shows that the unemployment rate dropped from 6.2% to 4.6%, which is a notable improvement. Additionally, the economy added over 400,000 new jobs in various sectors. This positive trend has been attributed to the successful vaccination rollout and the reopening of businesses as COVID-19 restrictions ease. Despite these improvements, challenges remain in certain industries like hospitality and tourism, which are still struggling to recover fully. Experts are hopeful that with continued vaccination efforts and government support, the economy will continue to strengthen and more jobs will be created.

news.headline: Lake Shore Bancorp, Inc. plans conversion and reorganization, moving from mutual to stock holding structure.
news.summary: In Dunkirk, NY on January 30, 2025, Lake Shore Bancorp, Inc. revealed plans for a significant change in its structure. The holding company for Lake Shore Savings Bank, listed on NASDAQ as LSBK, shared that its parent mutual holding company, Lake Shore, MHC, approved a Plan of Conversion and Reorganization. This move will involve a "second step" conversion from mutual holding to stock holding company status. As part of this transition, the Bank will seek approval to change its charter to become a New York-chartered commercial bank.

news.headline: Big banks may need to embrace crypto despite their reservations, as "debanking" becomes a priority for the Trump White House.
news.summary: The Republican party has intensified efforts towards the process of "debanking," which has now become a key focus for the Trump administration.

news.headline: Wingstop's stock drops 12% as profit misses estimates.
Chinese AI startup challenges US dominance, causing investors to doubt inflated stock market surge fueled by Trump.
news.summary: On Monday, a minor Chinese company posed a challenge to America's dominance in artificial intelligence technology, causing concern among investors about the potential overvaluation of the stock market due to the surge under the Trump administration.

news.headline: Comark reports going concern deals for Cleo, Ricki’s, and Bootlegger, aiming to save 530 jobs.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was disclosed that the country's economy grew by 5.2% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations. The unemployment rate also dropped to 3.8%, the lowest in five years. Additionally, inflation remained steady at 2.1%. Experts predict this continued growth trend will lead to increased consumer spending and business investments in the coming months.

news.headline: Azerion sells additional bonds, raising EUR 50M.
3 scholars win Nobel economics prize for research on national wealth.
Political storm looms over Trump as he faces allegations and investigations.
Roquette completes EUR 1.2 billion bond offering for IFF Pharma Solutions acquisition.
news.summary: Roquette has recently finished its first ever bond offering, raising a total of EUR 1.2 billion. The funds will be utilized to support the substantial acquisition of IFF Pharma Solutions.

news.headline: Kazatomprom sees production surge by 17% in Q3.
January sees a 1.5% decline in building permits issued.
Duolingo rated buy with $222 target at Seaport.
Housing construction drops by 14.8% in January following a revised 3.3% increase in December.
Vestas Wind Systems stock drops due to company projecting margins to be at lower end of estimate.
Congress facing pressure to address housing costs, with no clear path to bipartisan compromise.
news.summary: Exactly one year ago, Congress was under pressure to tackle the exorbitant costs facing homeowners and renters. However, there was no evident route to reach a bipartisan agreement on the matter.

news.headline: President Biden signed the Social Security Fairness Act into law, a victory for public sector unions after decades of push, on Sunday.
news.summary: On Sunday, President Joe Biden fulfilled a long-standing goal of public sector unions by signing the Social Security Fairness Act into law. This achievement marks a significant milestone after years of advocacy efforts.

news.headline: Azerion lists EUR 50 million Subsequent Bonds on Nasdaq Stockholm.
news.summary: This information is not intended for distribution to any individual in the United States or to any U.S. person under Regulation S of the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended. It should also not be distributed in any other jurisdiction where such action is unlawful.

news.headline: President will unveil reciprocal tariffs next week on countries taxing U.S. products. Suspended action against China; its low-cost products can enter U.S. duty-free.
news.summary: The president announced intentions to impose tariffs on countries that impose taxes on U.S. products. Additionally, he suspended planned tariffs on China, enabling the re-entry of their affordable products into the U.S. without tariffs.

news.headline: Foremost Clean Energy clarifies effective date of spin out of gold and silver properties into Rio Grande Resources Ltd. on Jan. 30, 2025.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 30, 2025, Foremost Clean Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ: FMST) (CSE: FAT), a North American uranium and lithium exploration company, addressed the recently released CSE bulletin regarding the anticipated effective date of the spin-out of its gold and silver properties. The spin-out, which involves transferring these assets into a new public company called Rio Grande Resources Ltd., was originally scheduled to take place on the same day. This move comes as part of a plan of arrangement and seeks to provide clarity on the company's future endeavors in the industry.

news.headline: Tariffs on Canada put on hold by Trump for 30 days, motives and resolution uncertain.
news.summary: The President declared imposing significant tariffs on Canada, but later decided to defer them for a month. The conditions needed to meet his approval are currently undisclosed.

news.headline: New all-electric Cadillac named Vistiq revealed by General Motors; offers 300-mile range.
February's S&P U.S. manufacturing index ends at 52.2, above initial 51.5 reading.
Notice: RCI Banque to redeem €850M subordinated bonds issued on 18 Nov 2019.
American stock futures and bond yields decline after reports of Putin updating nuclear doctrine.
Supporters of Biden's non-compete ban push for reversal of lower court blockade at 5th Circuit Appeals.
news.summary: Advocates for the Biden administration's almost complete prohibition of non-compete agreements are calling on the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals to reverse a previous court decision that halted the Federal Trade Commission's regulation.

news.headline: Trump criticizes Zelenskyy, US spending $200 billion on Ukraine conflict. Traders predict 23% chance of Zelenskyy leaving office before July 2025, 29% chance of Trump ending Ukraine war in 90 days if elected president.
news.summary: President Donald Trump has harshly criticized Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, accusing him of outmaneuvering former U.S. president Joe Biden. This has led to speculation about Zelenskyy’s political future, with a 23% chance of him leaving office before July 2025 according to Polymarket data. There is also a 29% probability that if elected president, Trump could end the Ukraine war within his first 90 days. Trump expressed his discontent with the U.S. spending on the conflict, highlighting that $200 billion has been invested. Full details are on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Powell suggests possible rate cut as soon as September, signaling economic concerns. #FedPolicy #SeptemberRateCut
Germany's services PMI drops to 49.4, lowest in 9 months.
Amazon reports record sales and items sold during best-ever Thanksgiving week.
Evome Medical reduces debt, strengthens finances with amendment to forbearance agreement with subsidiaries and Mirion.
news.summary: On Feb. 10, 2025, Evome Medical Technologies Inc. (TSXV: EVMT) from Shirley, N.Y., revealed a notable decrease in its total debt through a new amendment to a forbearance agreement. This move with Biodex Rehab Systems, LLC, Biodex Medical Systems, Inc., and Mirion Technologies (US), Inc. has significantly improved the Company's financial standing. The agreement, dated August 4, 2023, has helped in enhancing the financial position of the Company by reducing its debt levels, showcasing a positive development for its stakeholders.
